codeReview.summary: The header parser now distinguishes supported and unsupported versions and the added tests cover the requested AC1015/AC1018 boundaries, but the feature is not cleanly attributable to the reported commit. The handoff cites commit cd9cde7, yet that commit only touched tests/read_headers.rs and does not include the parser change in src/file_header.rs that the feature depends on.

issues.description: The reviewed feature cannot be traced to the reported commit. The handoff and review metadata point to commit cd9cde7, but git show cd9cde7 --stat -- crates/h7cad-native-dwg/src/file_header.rs crates/h7cad-native-dwg/tests/read_headers.rs shows only test-file changes; the parser behavior change in section_count_offset() is outside that commit, so the feature lacks auditable implementation provenance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dwg-parser-worker skill does not give a workable path for success reporting when the workspace is shared and dirty, yet EndFeatureRun requires a commitId. The worker reused an unrelated prior commit ID because there was no documented no-commit path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=false and says cd9cde7 was reused because creating an isolated commit was unsafe. AGENTS.md documents shared dirty workspace constraints, but .factory/skills/dwg-parser-worker/SKILL.md step 7 still assumes a concrete commit-backed handoff.

summary: Fail: behavior and tests look correct in the current worktree, but the feature review fails because the reported commit provenance is incorrect and does not contain the parser-side implementation it claims to review.
